name: Incident Triage
description: Hero AIOps flow. Given an alert/symptom + namespace + time window, fans the trigger out to THREE parallel built-in MCP calls — openagentic_prometheus.prometheus_query (metrics), openagentic_loki.loki_search_errors (logs), openagentic_kubernetes.k8s_list_pods (cluster state) — converges them in a labeled merge keyed by source-node label (metrics/logs/kube), pre-computes per-source evidence excerpts + HTML rows in a single correlate transform, asks the platform LLM (Smart Router, model:auto) to produce a root-cause narrative that CITES the concrete evidence (which metric / which log line / which pod fact) behind each conclusion, strips any chain-of-thought preamble, and renders an HTML incident-triage report as an openable artifact. No hardcoded model id or provider anywhere.

meta.purpose: Triage an alert/symptom by correlating metrics, logs, and Kubernetes state pulled IN PARALLEL, then produce an evidence-cited root-cause narrative.
how_it_works: 1. Fans the trigger out to three parallel MCP calls: openagentic_prometheus.prometheus_query (instant PromQL on the symptom expression), openagentic_loki.loki_search_errors (recent errors in the affected namespace/window), openagentic_kubernetes.k8s_list_pods (current pod state).
2. A labeled merge converges the three branches keyed by snake-cased source-node label (metrics / logs / kube).
3. A single correlate transform normalizes each branch into text excerpts + pre-computes the evidence table HTML rows.
4. The platform LLM (model:auto) writes a root-cause narrative that cites the concrete metric value, log signal, and pod fact behind each conclusion.
5. A CoT-stripper transform removes any LLM preamble.

6. webhook_response renders the HTML triage report and persists it as an openable artifact. Quiet-cluster short-circuit: when all three sources are clean, the report says so instead of inventing a cause.
meta.expected_output: Interactive HTML artifact: alert header, a root-cause narrative with inline [metric]/[log]/[k8s] evidence citations, and an evidence table (metric result, top error log lines, pod state) the operator can verify against.
meta.useful_when: Oncall pages on an alert/symptom and you need a fast, evidence-backed first hypothesis before deep-diving — the headline demo of parallel-MCP correlation.

defaultInputs.alert: High error rate on the api service

operations.value: {{ (() => { const t = String((input.logs && input.logs.content) || ''); const lines = t.split(/\r?\n/).filter(l => l && l.trim()); return lines.slice(0, 20).join('\n').slice(0, 2000); })() }}

{{ (() => { const b = input && input.kube; if (!b || typeof b !== 'object') return []; if (Array.isArray(b.pods)) return b.pods; if (typeof b.content === 'string') { try { const p = JSON.parse(b.content).pods; return Array.isArray(p) ? p : []; } catch (e) { return []; } } return []; })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.kube_array) ? input.kube_array : []; return a.filter(p => p && (String(p.status || '') !== 'Running' || (typeof p.restarts === 'number' && p.restarts > 5))).slice(0, 10).map(p => ({ name: String(p.name || ''), status: String(p.status || ''), restarts: (typeof p.restarts === 'number' ? p.restarts : 0) })); })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.kube_array) ? input.kube_array : []; return a.length; })() }}

{{ (() => { const esc = s => String(s == null ? '' : s).replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); const rows = []; rows.push(`metric
${esc(String(input.metrics_text || 'no metric result').slice(0, 500))}
`); rows.push(`logs
${esc(String(input.logs_text || 'no error logs in window').slice(0, 500))}
`); const up = Array.isArray(input.unhealthy_pods) ? input.unhealthy_pods : []; const kubeCell = up.length === 0 ? `all ${(typeof input.pod_total === 'number' ? input.pod_total : 0)} pods Running` : up.map(p => `${p.name} (${p.status}, restarts=${p.restarts})`).join('\n'); rows.push(`k8s
${esc(kubeCell)}
`); return rows.join(''); })() }}

{{ (() => { const m = String(input.metrics_text || '').trim(); const noMetric = !m || /Results:\s*0/i.test(m); const noLogs = !String(input.logs_text || '').trim(); const noUnhealthy = (Array.isArray(input.unhealthy_pods) ? input.unhealthy_pods.length : 0) === 0; if (noMetric && noLogs && noUnhealthy) return '

All clear — metrics, logs, and pod state are clean for this window. No root cause to assign; recommend confirming the alert is not stale.

'; return ''; })() }}

data.label: Root-Cause Narrative
data.system: You are an SRE doing incident triage. You correlate three evidence sources — Prometheus metrics, Loki error logs, and Kubernetes pod state — into a single root-cause hypothesis. You NEVER invent evidence. Every claim must cite which source supports it using inline markers [metric], [log], or [k8s]. If the evidence does not support a cause, you say the signal is inconclusive rather than guessing.
